claim_bullets.bullet_text: The game board is hidden. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-264)
Player calls out directions as the chequer moves. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-264)

source_record_text: The problem involves a finite checkerboard of unknown size, where arrows point to one of four sides. A chequer is placed randomly on the board, and each move consists of moving the chequer one square in the direction of the arrow in the square it occupies. If the arrow should cause the chequer to move off the edge of the board, the game halts.
